label: Localized shingles without eye or neurologic complication
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized shingles or herpes zoster.
No eye, face, ear, neurologic, disseminated, systemic, pregnancy, or immunocompromised-host complication documented.
exclusion_criteria: Rash on the face, near the eye, ear involvement, vision symptoms, neurologic symptoms, meningitis or encephalitis concern.
Disseminated rash, severe systemic illness, pneumonia concern, pregnancy, immunocompromised host, or uncontrolled pain.
Specialist-directed ophthalmology, neurology, or infectious-disease plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or dermatology as instructed, especially if pain persists after the rash improves.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports shingles and herpes zoster framing, painful blistering rash, keeping rash clean and covered, avoiding high-risk contacts until crusting, clinician-directed medicines, and urgent escalation for eye, neurologic, systemic, disseminated, or high-risk-host complications. Autonomous Phase 1551-1600 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed localized shingles without eye, face, ear, neurologic, disseminated, systemic, pregnancy, immunocompromised-host, or specialist-directed complication pathway.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype.
